title: Elvis
writer: BAZ LUHRMANN & SAM BROMELL and BAZ LUHRMANN & CRAIG PEARCE and JEREMY DONER
logline: The rise and fall of Elvis Presley as told through the eyes of his enigmatic manager Colonel Tom Parker, exploring their complex relationship from Elvis's discovery to his tragic end in Las Vegas.
antagonistOrConflict: Colonel Tom Parker and the exploitative entertainment industry

actBreaks.description: Elvis signs with Colonel Parker and achieves massive success, ending with his transformation into a caged performer in the ghostly Vegas casino.

validation.ruling: This scene shows Elvis caged in microphones performing 'Heartbreak Hotel' as headlines proclaim his success, marking the irreversible transformation from unknown performer to major recording star and fundamentally changing the story's direction from struggle to fame.

actBreaks.description: The 1968 NBC Special marks Elvis's artistic rebirth and temporary escape from the Colonel's control before returning to Vegas entrapment.

validation.ruling: Colonel walks away from the Elvis set back into the ghostly casino, admitting he was wrong about the '68 Special being a disaster, showing him at his most vulnerable - this marks the irreversible shift where Elvis has proven his independence and artistic power.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated framing device with the dying Colonel Parker as unreliable narrator, creating a non-linear structure that weaves between 1997 Vegas hospital scenes and flashbacks spanning 1947-1977. The story functions as both confession and self-justification, with the ghostly Vegas casino serving as a liminal space where past and present collide. The structure mirrors Elvis's own entrapment - circular, repetitive, always returning to Vegas despite attempts to break free. Luhrmann uses rapid-fire montages and time jumps to create a fever-dream quality that reflects both the Colonel's deteriorating mental state and the whirlwind nature of Elvis's career.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically after the Ferris wheel scene (scenes 57-62) where Elvis makes his Faustian bargain. The middle section slows during the army years and movie period (scenes 107-133), creating a sense of artistic stagnation, before exploding back to life with the 1968 NBC Special sequence (scenes 139-178). The final act maintains relentless momentum as Elvis spirals toward his demise, with increasingly frantic cross-cutting between performance and personal destruction.

incitingIncident.description: Scotty Moore and Bill Black prepare to perform with Elvis (misspelled as 'Ellis' on the poster) while Dixie brings Elvis a Pepsi to calm his nerves before their newcomer's spot.

validation.ruling: Scene #15 is merely a transitional moment showing carnies packing up. Scene #20 shows the Colonel's first encounter with Elvis performing at the Hayride, which shatters his ordinary world as a carnival operator and forces him to pursue this extraordinary talent, establishing the main conflict and goal of their relationship.

midpoint.description: A sign reads 'NBC Color City' outside the NBC Studios.

validation.ruling: Scene #139 is just an establishing shot of NBC studios. Scene #138 shows Elvis sitting on the dilapidated Hollywood sign, revealing his broken dreams of being a great actor like James Dean - this is the key revelation and moment of truth that shifts him from reactive to proactive, leading to his transformation in the '68 Comeback Special.

climax.description: Elvis confronts Colonel in the parking garage, calling him a blood-sucking vampire, while Colonel offers to personally loan him the money he owes.

validation.ruling: Scene #240 shows Colonel dictating bills in his office, which is setup rather than confrontation. Scene #247 depicts the ultimate confrontation in the parking garage where Elvis directly faces Colonel about his lies and manipulation, representing the peak dramatic tension and final unavoidable confrontation between protagonist and antagonist.

scenes.heading: INT. ORDINARY APT. (VEGAS) - LATE AFTERNOON (1997)
scenes.summary: An elderly, frail Colonel Tom Parker adjusts a radio in his Vegas apartment, climbs a ladder to retrieve Christmas cards, falls to the floor, and begins narrating his story.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAS VEGAS STRIP - DUSK
scenes.summary: An ambulance races through Las Vegas as a radio announcer reports that Colonel Tom Parker has been rushed to Valley Hospital.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Inside the ambulance, medics attempt to revive the unconscious Colonel as he continues his voiceover defense.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - DUSK
scenes.summary: The ambulance passes the International Hotel as the camera spins back in time from the Star Trek sign to an Elvis sign from the 1970s.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - PORTE COCHERE - NIGHT (1974)
scenes.summary: Elvis fans pour into the International Hotel in 1974 while the Colonel's voiceover acknowledges accusations of exploitation and drug enabling.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SERVICE CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A drowned man with long black hair floats underwater in a hotel service corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A warm-up comedian performs cheesy jokes onstage in the hotel showroom.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - CASINO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the casino, security guard Red West escorts Tom Diskin to whisper in the Colonel's ear near a craps table stacked with chips.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SERVICE CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis is pulled from a bucket of ice water by his hair, collapses on the floor while bodyguards, Jerry Schilling, Dr. Nick, and the Colonel assess his condition.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis's physical collapse reveals the severity of his condition and sets up the central crisis

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The comedian onstage runs out of material and stalls for time as the restless crowd grows impatient.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SERVICE CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vernon asks Dr. Nick what he can do for Elvis as the doctor opens his bag of medications, pills, and syringes to prepare an injection.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis transforms into a superhero-like figure, donning his white jumpsuit, belt, guns, jewelry, and cape while singing 'Glory! Glory! Hallelujah!'

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The hotel sign transforms back to the Star Trek Experience as the scene jumps back to the present.

scenes.heading: INT. VALLEY HOSPITAL - COLONEL’S ROOM - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: The Colonel jolts awake in his hospital bed, denies killing Elvis, stands with his IV stand, and shuffles down the corridor while defending himself as Elvis's promoter, not manager.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colonel's hospital confession and defense establishes the narrative frame and his perspective on the story

scenes.heading: EXT. CARNIVAL - DAY (1955)
scenes.summary: Carnies pack up a carnival in 1955 as Jimmie Rodgers Snow excitedly shows off an Elvis record to a teenager in a truck cab.
scenes.turningPointNote: First introduction to the Colonel's world and the beginning of Elvis's rise to fame

scenes.heading: EXT. CARNIVAL - CHOW TENT - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: The 45-year-old Colonel plans Hank Snow's tour with Tom Diskin, organizing police escorts, carnival weddings, and parades while directing his carnival crew.

scenes.heading: EXT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The camera establishes the exterior of the Shreveport Hayride auditorium at night with Jimmie Rodgers Snow singing inside.

scenes.heading: INT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jimmie Rodgers Snow performs onstage with cowboys and a cowgirl while Hank Snow and Diskin watch approvingly from the back of the auditorium.

scenes.heading: INT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Colonel gathers intelligence backstage from Horace Logan about a new singer on Sun Records who appeals to pop, country, and Black audiences.

scenes.heading: EXT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - ALLEY - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Scotty Moore and Bill Black prepare to perform with Elvis (misspelled as 'Ellis' on the poster) while Dixie brings Elvis a Pepsi to calm his nerves before their newcomer's spot.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHAKE RAG - AFTERNOON (1947)
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Elvis, wearing a cardboard lightning bolt like his comic book hero Captain Marvel Jr., looks toward a Pentecostal tent in the segregated Shake Rag neighborhood.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Young Elvis peers through a spyhole into a juke joint where Big Boy Crudup plays guitar and a customer dances erotically with a bordello girl.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHAKE RAG - JUKE JOINT - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The shocked boys fight over the spyhole as Young Elvis pulls away from the overwhelming scene.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHAKE RAG - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Young Elvis bolts away from the juke joint toward the Pentecostal tent as the other boys give chase.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTECOSTAL TENT - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Young Elvis moves forward in the Pentecostal tent with arms aloft, becoming one with the spiritual music as the other boys watch from outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - BACK ALLEY - NIGHT (1955)
scenes.summary: The Colonel glimpses the adult Elvis's heavy-lidded, mascaraed eyes as he sings 'I'll Fly Away' in the alley behind the Hayride auditorium.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTECOSTAL TENT - AFTERNOON (1947)
scenes.summary: The Pastor stops Smoky from interrupting Young Elvis's spiritual rapture in the tent as sacred and profane music blend together.
scenes.turningPointNote: The fusion of sacred and profane music establishes Elvis's unique artistic identity

scenes.heading: EXT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - ALLEY - NIGHT (1955)
scenes.summary: Elvis duets across time with Big Boy Crudup as Sam Phillips and Marion Keisker arrive with news of radio excitement, while the Colonel follows them inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUN STUDIOS - DUSK (1954)
scenes.summary: Marion crosses to Sun Studios carrying coffee and donuts as the Colonel's voiceover explains Sam Phillips's mission to record Black music and find a white country star.

scenes.heading: INT. SUN STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - DUSK
scenes.summary: Marion brings coffee to the despondent Sam Phillips in the control room while Elvis plays around in the studio, prompting Marion to defend him as 'different.'

scenes.heading: INT. SUN STUDIOS - RECORDING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Sam repositions the microphone as Elvis sings 'That's Alright, Mama' with Scotty and Bill joining in to shape the track over hours of recording.
scenes.turningPointNote: The creation of Elvis's breakthrough recording that will change everything

scenes.heading: INT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - NIGHT (1955)
scenes.summary: Elvis and the boys enter the Hayride auditorium as the Colonel positions himself near the loading dock and climbs to the fly tower above the stage.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTECOSTAL TENT - AFTERNOON (1947)
scenes.summary: Young Elvis reaches a powerful crescendo in the Pentecostal tent as Horace Logan's voice announces Elvis Presley to tepid applause.

scenes.heading: INT. "HAYRIDE” AUDITORIUM - STAGE - NIGHT (1955)
scenes.summary: Twenty-year-old Elvis takes the Hayride stage in a pink jacket and black lace, his heavily made-up face and architectural hair shocking the conservative Southern audience as he nervously speaks with Horace Logan.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis's first major performance that will transform American culture

scenes.heading: EXT. MEMPHIS, TENNESSEE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Colonel's voiceover declares Elvis was his destiny as the camera establishes Memphis, Tennessee and the Mississippi River.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUDERDALE COURTS (MEMPHIS) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis's recording of 'Blue Moon' plays on radios throughout the Lauderdale Courts housing project as a Crown Electric truck arrives.

scenes.heading: INT. CROWN ELECTRIC TRUCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis glimpses his eyes in the truck's rear-view mirror and picks up his guitar.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUDERDALE COURTS - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis walks through the Courts in his Crown Electric uniform as residents react with confusion to his sudden notoriety, some mocking, others intrigued.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESLEY APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Gladys anxiously watches from the window as Elvis arrives home, while Vernon discusses Elvis's performance on the phone and Dixie helps prepare dinner.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUDERDALE COURTS - STAIRWELL - EVENING
scenes.summary: Elvis serenades Dixie with 'Blue Moon' in the stairwell while holding a trash can, then becomes distracted thinking about Hank Snow's personal invitation.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESLEY APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Gladys confronts Elvis about quitting his job to tour while the family gathers for dinner, leading to a heated argument about his lifestyle choices and keeping the Sabbath.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESLEY APARTMENT - BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Gladys takes medication in the bedroom as Elvis promises to buy her a pink Cadillac, while she expresses fear not of his failure but of his success changing him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gladys's prophetic fear about success destroying Elvis establishes the tragic trajectory

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUDERDALE COURTS - DAY
scenes.summary: The family waves goodbye as Elvis, Scotty, and Bill drive away from Lauderdale Courts to join the tour.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: The Colonel spins a roulette wheel that transforms into a spinning hubcap as his sedan overtakes Elvis's convoy on a rural road, heading to the Tasty Grill.

scenes.heading: EXT. SMALL COUNTRY DINER - DAY
scenes.summary: The tour convoy parks outside a small country diner.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL COUNTRY DINER - DAY
scenes.summary: The Colonel enters the diner where Hank Snow confronts him about Elvis being on the tour, while the Colonel checks the jukebox and defends Elvis as coming from a good Christian family.

scenes.heading: INT. AUDITORIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis breaks two guitar strings during his explosive performance, driving teenage fans wild with his sexual movements while Hank Snow watches in horror with the Colonel and Jimmie.

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL - ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis talks to Dixie on the phone in his motel room, reassuring her about their relationship, then opens the door to find a female fan who seduces him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis's first infidelity marks his moral corruption and distance from home

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CHEVY BEL AIR - DAY
scenes.summary: Scotty drives while a despondent Elvis considers taking a white pill offered by Bill to 'put the pep back in your step.'
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis's first pill marks the beginning of his drug dependency

scenes.heading: EXT. DAYTONA CARNIVAL - STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis and the boys flee the stage through a shower of panties as Jimmie expresses his admiration, while Hank Snow tells the Colonel he can no longer appear with Elvis.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank Snow's rejection forces the Colonel to choose between his established star and Elvis

scenes.heading: EXT. CARNY CAMP - BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the carny camp backstage, Hank Snow prays in his trailer then emerges disgusted by a party, while Elvis stands apart gazing at the Ferris wheel, perturbed.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: In ghostly Vegas 1997, Old Colonel narrates seeing Elvis alone and lost as his claw nearly touches Elvis' shoulder before being interrupted.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARNY CAMP - NIGHT (1955)
scenes.summary: Colonel stands unseen behind Elvis as Scotty calls for Elvis to join the group heading toward an illuminated clown's mouth.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. CARNIVAL - HALL OF MIRRORS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis enters the Hall of Mirrors under Roy Lumous' watchful gaze as Colonel appears in a mirror reflection and speaks to Lumous in carny language.

scenes.heading: INT. CARNIVAL - HALL OF MIRRORS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lumous closes a mirrored door, magically separating Elvis from his friends, leaving him alone and lost when Colonel appears asking if he's lost.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARNIVAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Colonel leads Elvis through the carnival, greeting carnies warmly and explaining carny language while revealing his orphan background and circus origins.

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRIS WHEEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the Ferris wheel, Colonel promises Elvis a blazing future with recording contracts and television, requiring exclusive representation and leaving Hank Snow.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colonel makes his pitch for exclusive representation, beginning his manipulation of Elvis

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRIS WHEEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Colonel tells Elvis that greatness requires sacrifices and freedom from entanglements as Elvis imagines breaking up with Dixie in a flash forward.

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRIS WHEEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Colonel explains needing the parents' commitment as a flash forward shows him convincing Vernon and Gladys to sign contracts for Elvis Presley Enterprises.

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRIS WHEEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis tells Colonel he'd do anything to keep his parents from poverty as a flash forward shows Gladys signing the contracts after Elvis asks her to say yes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The contracts are signed, sealing Elvis' fate with Colonel Parker

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRIS WHEEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis and Colonel playfully escalate their dreams from buying Cadillacs to rocket ships, with Elvis expressing his desire to fly despite his mother's fears.

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRIS WHEEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: As the Ferris wheel turns, Elvis and Colonel appear to be flying through the air.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: In ghostly Vegas, Elvis performs in a cage of microphones as executives call his music morbid while headlines dissolve showing his rise to number one.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRACELAND - GATES - DAY (1956)
scenes.summary: Elvis arrives at Graceland in a pink Cadillac, presenting the keys to Gladys as they cross the threshold of their new mansion.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel unveils merchandise featuring Elvis' face on cushions, perfume, lipsticks, and plaster busts as boxes are unloaded into Graceland.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEALE STREET (MEMPHIS) - DAY (1954)
scenes.summary: A hate badge transforms into a flower on a mural outside Lansky Bros. as the camera reveals the thriving hub of Black culture on Beale Street.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CLUB HANDY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Big Mama Thornton rehearses singing in Club Handy through a second-story window as the camera descends.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEALE STREET (MEMPHIS) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis in his Crown Electric uniform walks through bustling Beale Street as Old Colonel narrates about Elvis seeking the look and styles of Black music.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. LANSKY BROS. - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elvis admires Lansky Bros. clothing store through the window as B.B. King is fitted inside, with Lansky showing B.B. to the door.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. LANSKY BROS. - DAY (1956)
scenes.summary: Now successful, Elvis shops at Lansky Bros. with B.B. King, buying up the store as Lansky tells him to buy from him, not buy him out.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS (LOS ANGELES) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis performs on NBC Studios as Colonel watches, predicting that tomorrow all of America will be talking about Elvis Presley.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Elvis' performance ripples out through 50 million TVs in ghostly Vegas as his voice fills the screen.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATOR EASTLAND’S HOUSE - FAMILY ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: An ideal American family watches Uncle Miltie on television while the father reads in his study surrounded by suited colleagues.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS (LOS ANGELES) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis' performance sends ripples of laughter through the predominantly female NBC audience.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - DAY
scenes.summary: At Graceland, Gladys, Vernon, Dixie and the extended Presley family crowd around their television as Gladys fondly recalls Elvis making her laugh.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATOR EASTLAND’S HOUSE - STUDY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Senator Eastland reads about segregation being struck down while colleagues discuss his racial integrity tour, distracted by his children's laughter from the TV.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS (LOS ANGELES) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis pushes his gyration further and further as his NBC performance takes on a more raw and intense tone.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATOR EASTLAND’S HOUSE - FAMILY ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Senator Eastland's face fills with rage as he watches Elvis, seeing his daughters' desire and catching the same look on his son's face.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Presley family whoops and hollers as Milton Berle kids around with Elvis, pretending to gyrate on television.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATOR EASTLAND’S HOUSE - FAMILY ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Eastland strides toward the television in white rage, his body shaking with hate before snapping the screen black, ending the fun.
scenes.turningPointNote: Eastland's violent reaction marks the beginning of organized opposition to Elvis

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Senator Eastland appears on 1950s TVs denouncing rock and roll as Old Colonel narrates that television made it political, which isn't good for business.

scenes.heading: EXT. HUDSON THEATER (NEW YORK) - DAY (1956)
scenes.summary: Outside NBC's Hudson Theater, massive crowds of reporters and teens gather as politicians denounce Elvis while his gospel voice is heard inside.

scenes.heading: INT. HUDSON THEATER - REHEARSAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis plays piano and sings gospel in a rehearsal room surrounded by newspapers with headlines calling him childish, including "Elvis the Pelvis belongs in the Jungle."

scenes.heading: INT. STEVE ALLEN STUDIOS - STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the Steve Allen show, Elvis in formal tails faces a live basset hound in a top hat as the flat audience doesn't react, creating a nightmare of humiliation.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVE ALLEN STUDIOS - REHEARSAL ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Elvis bursts into the rehearsal room tearing off his tails in fury, arguing with Scotty about the humiliating performance that made fun of them.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRACELAND - FRONT GATES - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis arrives at Graceland's gates visibly shaken, seeing fans holding signs reading "No New Elvis" and "We Want Old Elvis."

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Presley family argues in the living room as Vernon changes TV channels and Gladys criticizes the New York people for using Elvis to mock the South.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRACELAND - DUSK
scenes.summary: Elvis peels out of Graceland in his purple Cadillac El Dorado at dusk.

scenes.heading: EXT. GATES OF GRACELAND - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis drives through the fans at Graceland's gates as a black sedan follows him from across the street.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. EL DORADO - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis flips through radio stations hearing his own songs repeatedly before landing on "Rip It Up" as he drives toward downtown Memphis.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEALE STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis parks his El Dorado on Beale Street as B.B. King spots him from Club Handy's second-story window and signals he'll help him avoid the crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. CLUB HANDY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: B.B. King leads Elvis through the ecstatic throng at Club Handy as Little Richard performs, with Elvis the only white face in the room.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEALE STREET - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Two shadowy figures in a car watch Elvis dancing with a young girl through Club Handy's window as the lights go off and gospel music begins.

scenes.heading: INT. CLUB HANDY - LATER
scenes.summary: After the crowd leaves, Elvis, B.B., Sister Rosetta, and Little Richard have a late-night gospel session as B.B. discusses control and business with Elvis on the fire escape.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: In ghostly Vegas, Senator Eastland confronts Colonel with photos of Elvis at Black venues and events, including Club Handy and the WDIA Goodwill Revue.

scenes.heading: EXT. RUSSWOOD PARK BASEBALL STADIUM - NIGHT (1956)
scenes.summary: At Russwood Park Baseball Stadium, the Vice Squad installs cameras while police erect barriers separating black and white teens as a patrol car arrives.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Senator Eastland appears before a Confederate flag declaring the South will retain segregation as Old Colonel narrates about the competing segregationist rally.

scenes.heading: INT. PATROL CAR - NIGHT (1956)
scenes.summary: Elvis sits in a police car with Colonel, dressed in black with red accents, as fans press against windows and Colonel advises ignoring the Vice Squad cameras.

scenes.heading: EXT. RUSSWOOD PARK BASEBALL STADIUM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Colonel guides Elvis through police corridors to his family as Gladys mentions Jesse is with them and Colonel gives final instructions to stand still and not wiggle.

scenes.heading: EXT. RUSSWOOD PARK STADIUM - STAGE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis takes the stage, quiets the crowd, and defiantly wiggles his pinky at the Vice Squad cameras before launching into "Hound Dog" to ear-shattering screams.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis openly defies authority and surveillance, choosing rebellion over compliance in a pivotal moment

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Senator Eastland speaks about using state police powers to prevent racial integration when he stops and turns toward the overwhelming noise of hysterical fans, realizing hordes are coming.

scenes.heading: EXT. RUSSWOOD PARK STADIUM - FIELD - NIGHT (1956)
scenes.summary: Police try to control the crowd at Russwood Park as Colonel tells the family to get back to the car for safety while Elvis becomes wildly provocative with RCA's mascot.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PATROL CAR/RUSSWOOD PARK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Police hustle Gladys and Elvis into separate cars as the stage collapses behind Colonel, with Elvis glimpsing his family departing in different directions through the rear window.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel violently upturns a table while shouting that Elvis didn't listen to him, as dramatic music builds with slot machine sounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRACELAND - DAY (1958)
scenes.summary: Drizzle smears the lonely Cadillacs in Graceland's driveway.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In the gloomy Graceland living room, Colonel tells the shaken family that Elvis faces either army service or jail, while revealing Vernon's past imprisonment for passing bad checks.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis is forced into military service, fundamentally changing his trajectory

scenes.heading: INT. BARBERSHOP (FORT CHAFFEE) - DAY (1958)
scenes.summary: Clippers cut off Elvis' hair at Fort Chaffee as his dark locks fall to the cement floor, with Elvis joking about being 'hair today, gone tomorrow.'

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A glass tumbler falls from Gladys' hand as she crumples to the ground in distress while Vernon stands helpless.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel approaches Gladys' casket in the distance as Elvis appears crying over his mother, while a choir sings a dark version of 'Heartbreak Hotel.'
scenes.turningPointNote: Gladys' death devastates Elvis and fundamentally changes his relationship with Colonel

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - MUSIC ROOM - DAY (1958)
scenes.summary: The extended Presley clan gathers around the casket as Elvis wails from the back of the house, while Colonel approaches the destroyed Vernon about getting press coverage.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - GLADYS’ BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Colonel finds Elvis inconsolable in Gladys' closet holding her dress, then manipulates him by promising to replace his mother's guidance and care while Elvis serves overseas.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colonel exploits Elvis' grief to gain complete control over him

scenes.heading: EXT. GRACELAND - FRONT STEPS - DAY
scenes.summary: Flashbulbs pop as Elvis and Vernon sit on Graceland's front steps surrounded by newspapermen and photographers for a press conference.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK HARBOR - DAY
scenes.summary: The U.S.S. Randall sails past the Statue of Liberty as a reporter's voice discusses Elvis joining the Army.

scenes.heading: INT. U.S.S. RANDALL - CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis lies in his bunk in military uniform with tears streaming down his face as he sings softly to himself about loneliness.

scenes.heading: EXT. OCEAN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The U.S.S. Randall appears tiny against the ocean horizon as the camera tilts up into the starry night.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Colonel lies on his back between casino blackjack tables under artificial stars as rain begins to fall, relieved that Elvis will return clean-cut but worried about the danger of love.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELVIS' HOUSE (BAD NAUHEIM) - DUSK (1959)
scenes.summary: Fans cluster around Elvis' modest two-story cottage in Bad Nauheim as the camera cranes up to discover a beribboned ponytail against a second-story window.

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS' HOUSE (BAD NAUHEIM) - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In Elvis' bedroom filled with fan mail and stuffed animals, Priscilla sits on the floor with Elvis in his military uniform, defending their relationship to others.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESS CONFERENCE - DAY (1960)
scenes.summary: At a press conference, Elvis tells interviewers that his big ambition is to become a dramatic actor, which takes time and experience but he hopes to make it.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel narrates over a montage of 1960s Elvis films, claiming Elvis was as good as Brando but audiences wanted him to sing, making his life one big Hollywood movie.

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS' VISTA-LINER BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis and Priscilla lie on a bed in their red velvet Vista-Liner bus speeding through the desert as 'Viva Las Vegas' plays and the Memphis Mafia pop out of every corner.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The gang attends Elvis and Priscilla's wedding in a hotel room as Old Colonel narrates about their fairytale wedding and honeymoon on Frank Sinatra's jet.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Priscilla cradles newborn baby Lisa Marie in a hospital as Old Colonel introduces her.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUNDSTAGE (REAR PROJECTION) - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis water-skis with three bikini-clad girls in front of rear-projection as production crew hurl water at his face, while Old Colonel narrates about making faster, cheaper films as the world changed with MLK's assassination.
scenes.turningPointNote: MLK's assassination marks a shift from Hollywood fantasy to harsh reality

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS' VISTA-LINER BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis learns lines on his bus when he notices Walter Cronkite announcing MLK's assassination in Memphis on television, rising in disbelief as Jerry turns up the volume.

scenes.heading: INT. MGM SOUNDSTAGE - ARCHIVAL FOOTAGE
scenes.summary: Elvis films 'Edge of Reality' in a blue pajama suit as the scene intercuts with archival footage of Vietnam, the Beatles, and Civil Rights movement, with headlines about his films bombing.

scenes.heading: EXT. MGM BACKLOT - DAY
scenes.summary: A golf cart travels through the MGM backlot toward Elvis' Vista-Liner surrounded by cars as the Memphis Mafia have a water fight, while Old Colonel narrates about money melting away.

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS’ VISTA-LINER BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Billy tells Elvis he's needed on set while Elvis sits devastated on the bus banquette watching Walter Cronkite report on Dr. King's death, telling Jerry that King always spoke the truth.

scenes.heading: EXT. MGM STUDIOS - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel pitches 'Elvis Presley's Wonderful World of Christmas' television special to Vernon and the Memphis Mafia using table models while Elvis holds baby Lisa Marie, looking at standees of himself with Singer products.

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS’ VISTA-LINER BUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis storms into his bus ripping off his collar as news of the King assassination aftermath plays on TV, while Billy tries to run lines for a ridiculous movie scene and Jerry announces he's leaving for a job in LA.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELVIS’ VISTA-LINER BUS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Priscilla sits in the limo outside the Vista-Liner as Billy packs away an Elvis standee and Charlie tells her Elvis is inside watching the Reverend's funeral.

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS’ VISTA-LINER BUS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis lies sprawled on his red velvet bed staring blankly at the muted television showing MLK's memorial while a Christmas special announcer plays on the tape recorder, tears forming in his eyes as Priscilla quietly enters.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Mahalia Jackson and a choir appear with Reverend Brewster at the pulpit, who tells them to sing when things are too dangerous to say, as they perform 'Joshua Fought the Battle of Jericho.'

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES - AERIAL SHOT - LATE AFTERNOON (1968)
scenes.summary: A convoy of two black cars and a blue limo cuts through the Hollywood Hills as heavy rock music continues.

scenes.heading: INT. LIMO - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Inside the limo, television producers Steve Binder and Bones Howe sit facing backwards, staring intensely at the blank-faced Sonny and Red West.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ANGELES - AERIAL SHOT - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The convoy threads the road above the derelict Hollywood sign as music builds.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOOKOUT ABOVE THE HOLLYWOOD SIGN - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The convoy stops near Jerry who leads the nervous Binder and Bones down a dusty dirt track after warning them not to call it a Christmas Special.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLLYWOOD SIGN - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis sits on the dilapidated Hollywood sign gazing toward Griffith Observatory, telling Binder and Bones about his dreams of being a great actor like James Dean and how everything feels broken down now.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis expresses his artistic frustration and desire for meaningful work, setting up the '68 Special

scenes.heading: EXT. NBC STUDIOS - DAY
scenes.summary: A sign reads 'NBC Color City' outside the NBC Studios.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis stands on the NBC stage with Priscilla and Jerry looking at empty bleachers as Jerry explains the taping schedule, with Elvis seeming disturbed about performing for a live audience.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Bones warms up the live audience filling the bleachers as the applause sign flashes.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel leads Singer Sewing executives through NBC corridors as a Christmas sweater is wheeled past, discussing the planned Christmas songs for the special.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: In the NBC control room, Colonel tells Binder they'll start with 'Here Comes Santa Claus' while the Singer executive mimes drumming for 'The Little Drummer Boy.'

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera tracks Elvis' black-leather-clad legs as he charges through the NBC corridor while Bones announces him to the audience.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE/CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis takes the NBC stage nervously to polite applause from an unfamiliar audience, missing his cue when he tries to start performing, creating confusion.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: In the control room, Colonel tells Binder to remove the audience and do the Christmas number as the Singer executives watch Elvis sweating and insecure on the monitors.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: The Dance Captain leads a group of Dancing Santas stretching and preparing to go on stage.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis looks to Priscilla in a do-or-die moment, cracks a joke that connects with the audience, then launches into a tremendous rocking version of 'Heartbreak Hotel' as Elvis Presley is reborn.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis reclaims his artistic power and connects with his audience, marking his comeback

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Colonel hurries down the stairs, huffing and puffing.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis performs 'Jailhouse Rock' to an erupting crowd while Priscilla watches in amazement.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Colonel rushes down stairs complaining about Elvis singing non-Christmas songs and discovers the Blue Moon Boys are present.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Elvis jokes with the audience and musicians during the sit-down section, pretending to leave and bantering with Charlie and Scotty.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Colonel storms into the control room demanding 'Here Comes Santa Claus' while Binder mentions Elvis will talk about himself.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis tosses aside the cue sheet and speaks from the heart about music, expressing his love for the music that makes him happy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis openly defies the scripted show and speaks authentically about his artistic passion

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: On the monitors, Elvis appears to look directly at Colonel as he declares his intention to sing the music he loves.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis looks to Priscilla and states he wants to sing music that makes him happy.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Binder watches Colonel's reaction as Elvis clearly takes his defiance personally.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel watches Elvis on monitors in the ghostly casino, feeling mocked and blamed as Elvis speaks about losing everything he cared about.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - DAY (1968)
scenes.summary: Elvis stares at Colonel from the stage while singing about feeling like a motherless child as Claude Thompson performs a dance solo.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel yells at the Floor Manager to start 'Here Comes Santa Claus' as Santas prepare and a wooden sleigh is positioned.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Binder announces the gospel and bordello numbers while Singer executives storm out threatening lawsuits over the deviation from Christmas content.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel spins among monitors showing Elvis in various dance numbers, overwhelmed by the chaotic spectacle that wasn't part of his plan.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - NIGHT (1968)
scenes.summary: The cast rushes from the stage as tragic news tears through them.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel stares at televisions showing the Kennedy tragedy from the Ambassador Hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - DRESSING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cast and crew gather around the TV watching news of Robert Kennedy's assassination while Binder gives a speech about their diverse unity.
scenes.turningPointNote: The assassination creates a pivotal moment that will reshape Elvis's artistic mission

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM
scenes.summary: Binder and Bones watch coverage of the Robert Kennedy shooting on monitors late at night.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC - DRESSING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Elvis confronts Colonel about the assassinations, declaring he's Elvis Presley and won't just sell sweaters while Colonel warns about Binder's influence.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis directly challenges Colonel's commercial priorities in the wake of national tragedy

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM/STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis stands alone on the abandoned studio floor contemplating while Binder asks about tomorrow's number from the control room.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Binder ponders and looks down at the script he was about to discard as piano chords begin.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - DRESSING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis lies on the floor reading lyrics to 'If I Can Dream' while Binder, Bones and Billy Goldenberg work on the song at the piano.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel arrives in Christmas sweater learning Elvis worked all night on a new song he plans to perform instead of the Christmas material.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis in white suit takes the stage after Priscilla's kiss as TV cameras turn from the Santa set to the illuminated 'ELVIS' letters.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Colonel moves in disbelief to the window as a camera crane sweeps in front of his reddening face.

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - STAGE - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis performs 'If I Can Dream' with soul-stirring power as the cast watches, moved by his message and voice.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis delivers his artistic and social statement through the powerful performance of 'If I Can Dream'

scenes.heading: INT. NBC STUDIOS - CONTROL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel's face shows thunder while Diskin shows inner approval as Priscilla enters and stands with Binder.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel walks away from the Elvis set back into the ghostly casino, admitting he was wrong about the performance being a disaster.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRACELAND - DAY (1969)
scenes.summary: Elvis and Priscilla race around on a golf cart during a lively picnic at Graceland in a new era.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel sits at the roulette table in catatonic despair, screaming about Elvis leaving him after one mistake.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - MUSIC ROOM - AFTERNOON (1969)
scenes.summary: Elvis, Priscilla and Tom Hulett discuss plans for a touring plane and international opportunities at a hip cocktail party in the reimagined Graceland.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND DINING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: At dinner, Elvis discusses his new song 'In The Ghetto' and album plans while Priscilla distributes jewelry gifts to the staff.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRACELAND - DAY
scenes.summary: An RCA mobile recording van is parked in Graceland's driveway with cables running into the house.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - JUNGLE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis records 'In The Ghetto' in the Jungle Room with backup singers and band while Priscilla watches from the stairs.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Archival images of riots play as Elvis's vocals from 'In The Ghetto' continue over the footage.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - JUNGLE ROOM - DAY (1969)
scenes.summary: Charlie answers Colonel's phone call while Priscilla shakes her head as Elvis finishes recording 'In The Ghetto' with authentic emotion.

scenes.heading: INT. ALADDIN CASINO (VEGAS) - GAMBLING FLOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel plays blackjack at the Aladdin Casino but the croupier refuses to deal him cards, directing him to see Mr. Kohn instead.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO - MEYER KOHN’S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Meyer Kohn and his intimidating associate confront Colonel about his gambling debts and threaten to make things uncomfortable.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - ELVIS' BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis tells Priscilla he must see Colonel in Vegas despite her warnings about being manipulated, insisting he owes him a face-to-face conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel walks through a European village model, pained by memories of a young woman's laughter and feeling sorry for him.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. LIMO/LAS VEGAS STRIP - DAY (1969)
scenes.summary: Elvis and the Memphis Mafia are driven past garish Las Vegas marquees including The Stardust, Caesars Palace, and The Flamingo.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The West cousins secure the hospital hallway as Diskin guides Elvis to Colonel's room.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - ROOM - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis visits Colonel in the hospital where he's recovering from a heart attack, though Colonel downplays it as just a back injury.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Colonel shows Elvis the International Hotel showroom and manipulates him by comparing his fear to artistic greatness while dismissing the international tour plans.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colonel successfully traps Elvis into the Vegas residency deal, ending his international touring dreams

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel watches a giant glittering 'ELVIS!' marquee being installed while telling gangsters that even desert gophers will know about the show.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis rehearses 'That's All Right' with the Sweet Inspirations and Imperials, playfully interacting with his backup singers and the Memphis Mafia.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel oversees his marketing campaign including hats, billboards, radio ads, and an army of giant stuffed hound dogs that he demands be made bigger.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - LATER
scenes.summary: Diskin presents Colonel with a bigger hound dog, but Colonel still demands it be made even larger.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis rehearses with the band, experimenting with karate moves and his unique style of movement as the music builds.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - FOYER - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel stands proudly in front of the 'World's Biggest Hound Dog' in the hotel foyer.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis rehearses with his band, directing the trombones and drums as Ronnie Tutt performs a thunderous drum solo.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rich and famous guests arrive at the International Hotel while merchandise sellers hawk to crowds outside.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis and his entourage walk backstage as the drum solo builds, with Elvis tuning his guitar and summoning his strength.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis takes the stage to deafening applause and launches into a fast version of 'That's All Right' as Colonel, Priscilla, Vernon and others watch from their booths.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - NIGHT (1947)
scenes.summary: Young Elvis peers through a peephole at a juke joint where Big Boy Crudup performs 'That's All Right' in 1947.

scenes.heading: INT. SUN RECORDS - STUDIO - NIGHT (1954)
scenes.summary: 19-year-old Elvis performs 'That's All Right' with Scotty and Bill at Sun Records in 1954.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT - DAY (1947)
scenes.summary: Big Boy Crudup continues singing at the juke joint as young Elvis mouths the words.

scenes.heading: INT. SUN RECORDS - STUDIO - NIGHT (1954)
scenes.summary: 19-year-old Elvis sings 'That's All Right' at Sun Records studio.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT (1969)
scenes.summary: Elvis performs 'That's All Right' at the International Hotel showroom in 1969.

scenes.heading: INT. JUKE JOINT/SUN STUDIOS/SHOWROOM - SPLIT SCREEN
scenes.summary: The three performers across time appear in split screen and merge into a big finish of 'That's All Right.'

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The audience erupts as Elvis banters between numbers while Colonel negotiates with Kohn and Goochera about making Elvis part of their family, discussing payment as Elvis performs 'Suspicious Minds.'

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis performs 'Suspicious Minds' as Colonel's cane tap triggers a stylized sequence, including a flash forward where four men charge the stage and Elvis reaches for a holster.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: 'Suspicious Minds' reaches its climax as Colonel observes Priscilla's fearful expression, realizing Elvis no longer belongs to just her.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis performs 'Can't Help Falling in Love' and connects with Colonel through a smile, then walks down into the audience.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Backstage, the Memphis Mafia tend to Elvis as Priscilla embraces him and Vernon expresses pride, saying their mother was looking down celebrating.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - PRESS CONFERENCE - LATER
scenes.summary: Elvis glows with pride at a post-show press conference, joking with reporters and discussing his longevity and enjoyment of the business.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - COLONEL’S SUITE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Colonel shows Jerry his new offices and merchandise setup, then explodes with rage when Jerry mentions the planned tour, ranting about security threats.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - ELVIS’ ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis takes pills and speaks softly on the phone with Priscilla about their daughter Lisa while buzzed from his show.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Priscilla asks when Elvis is coming home, appearing crestfallen on her end of the phone call.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - ELVIS' ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Elvis hangs up on Priscilla and asks a woman named Diane to stay with him, becoming paranoid about noises upstairs and fearing someone might kill him.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - DAWN
scenes.summary: Workmen lower the 'ELVIS!' sign from the International Hotel marquee at dawn.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL (VEGAS) - DAY
scenes.summary: In a Vegas hospital, Colonel shows newspaper articles about the Manson murders to justify canceling international tours while Elvis lies in bed with family around him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TARMAC - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis' jet taxis to a halt on the tarmac.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: A presidential-scale motorcade with dozens of police cars moves through the streets.

scenes.heading: EXT. VENUE - STAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis performs 'Burning Love' on stage flanked by two rows of cops, appearing jittery as a girl rushes the stage and is taken out by security.

scenes.heading: INT. VENUE - BACKSTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis is hustled into a car backstage as 'Burning Love' continues.

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS' LIMO (AMIDST THE MOTORCADE) - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis gulps pills in his limo surrounded by the Memphis Mafia as Dr. Nick injects him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TARMAC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Memphis Mafia usher Elvis from his limousine up the stairs of his waiting jet.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL (VEGAS) - DAY
scenes.summary: In the Vegas hospital, Elvis nods at the security plan and asks about going international while Colonel promises the Tokyo Opera House.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. TARMAC/MOTORCADE/VENUE/BACKSTAGE
scenes.summary: The cycle of tarmac, motorcade, venue, and backstage repeats in multiple cities shown in split screen.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis performs at the International through multiple years, practicing karate and bedding groupies in a drug-fueled cycle, changing jumpsuits between concerts.

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - BEDROOM/HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Priscilla packs jewelry into a valise as Elvis wakes up groggy, and she announces she's leaving the marriage and taking Lisa with her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Priscilla leaves Elvis, marking the collapse of his personal life

scenes.heading: INT. GRACELAND - HALLWAY/STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Priscilla confronts Elvis on the staircase, demanding a husband and father for Lisa, saying she has nothing left to give him.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - COLONEL’S SUITE - DAY
scenes.summary: Jerry confronts Tom Diskin about Elvis needing an international tour instead of Vegas repetition, questioning why Colonel won't let him go overseas.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - JERRY’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jerry finds an opened fan mail letter in his hotel room addressed to 'Uncle Andreas' asking why he doesn't return home to clear the family name.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Colonel walks through a model of Breda as the letter continues, then speaks directly to the audience about being born in Holland and whether past mistakes should damn someone forever.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL - SERVICE CORRIDOR - NIGHT (1974)
scenes.summary: Jerry urgently tells Elvis that Colonel lied about international tours because he can't leave the country and doesn't exist, causing Elvis to collapse.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis discovers Colonel's true identity and physical limitations, leading to his collapse

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elvis performs 'Battle Hymn of the Republic' in an American Eagle cape, reaching for divine notes as gospel choirs join him, then addresses the audience with house lights up.

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel watches 'Nightmare Alley' on multiple televisions, comparing Elvis to the alcoholic carnival performer who needed his bottle to perform.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - COLONEL’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Colonel calls to fire Dr. Nick and dictates to Diskin what Presley Enterprises owes as Elvis prepares to leave on his own.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRSTRIP - TARMAC - DAY
scenes.summary: The Lisa Marie jet drifts into frame on the tarmac.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - ELVIS’ ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis' Vegas suite is being packed up with drop cloths over furniture and gold records removed as Vernon enters looking ashen.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - PARKING GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Luggage is ferried into limousines in the hotel parking garage.

scenes.heading: INT. ELVIS’ LIMO - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis waits jittery in his limo, energized by withdrawal, but Charlie tells him Vernon won't come down and needs to talk.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - ELVIS’ ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Vernon shows Elvis a balance sheet revealing they owe $9,800,099 to Colonel and are broke despite years of Vegas performances.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis discovers he's financially trapped and broke despite his success

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - ELEVATOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis descends in the elevator, reining in his emotions as a powder keg, saying he's going to fly away.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL - PARKING GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elvis confronts Colonel in the parking garage, calling him a blood-sucking vampire, while Colonel offers to personally loan him the money he owes.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SUNSET
scenes.summary: The elevator travels up the hotel facade as 'Are You Lonesome Tonight' builds.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - ELVIS’ ROOM - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Elvis stands alone in his hotel room staring out at the Vegas sunset as 'Are You Lonesome Tonight' plays, described as the loneliest man in the world.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - COLONEL’S SUITE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Colonel sits in his suite staring at the telephone as 'Are You Lonesome Tonight' continues.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - ELVIS’ ROOM - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Elvis sits at piano singing quietly to himself, then tells Vernon with heartbroken disgust to tell 'that sonofabitch' he wants things back to the way they were, closes curtains and turns on TVs as he flops into his lounge.

scenes.heading: EXT. TARMAC - YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: Vernon watches from outside Elvis' limo as Priscilla arrives, takes Lisa Marie to her mother who then hands the child to a nanny before heading toward Elvis' car.

scenes.heading: INT. LIMO - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Priscilla enters Elvis' limo and after tense silence he sings 'I Will Always Love You' but stops mid-song, she suggests he go to a rest facility in San Diego but he doesn't respond.

scenes.heading: EXT. TARMAC - CONTINUOUS ACTION
scenes.summary: Elvis and Priscilla exit the limo on opposite sides, pause to look at each other across the roof, then Elvis mouths the final lyric 'I will always love you' before heading to his plane as they part ways.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final separation between Elvis and Priscilla - the end of their relationship

scenes.heading: INT. CASINO (GHOSTLY VEGAS) - NIGHT (1997)
scenes.summary: Old Colonel wanders into a casino and sits at a slot machine, recounting how the day Elvis died he immediately called the record company to print more records and told Vernon at the funeral 'let's get to work.'

scenes.heading: INT. INTERNATIONAL HOTEL - SHOWROOM - NIGHT (1977)
scenes.summary: Elvis performs his final concert, shockingly overweight and slurring, sits at piano to perform 'Unchained Melody' as his voice soars despite his physical deterioration.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elvis' final performance - the culmination of his tragic decline

structuralWarnings.text: All 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
